tools.name: list_interfaces
tools.title: List Interfaces
tools.description: Lists all interfaces on the MikroTik device (ethernet, bridge, WireGuard, PPPoE, VLAN, WiFi, SFP, LTE, loopback, and any other type).

tools.name: get_interface
tools.title: Get Interface
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific interface by name.

name.description: Exact interface name, e.g. 'ether1', 'wg0'

tools.title: Enable Interface
tools.description: Enables an interface on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: disable_interface
tools.title: Disable Interface
tools.description: Disables an interface on the MikroTik device.

tools.description: Lists VLAN interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.description: Updates an existing VLAN interface's settings on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_vlan_interface
tools.title: Remove VLAN
tools.description: Removes a VLAN interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.description: Creates a bridge interface on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_bridge_hosts
tools.title: List Bridge Hosts
tools.description: Lists the bridge host (MAC address) table on the MikroTik device.

tools.description: Adds a VLAN entry to a bridge's VLAN table (requires vlan-filtering on the bridge).

tools.name: get_poe_monitor
tools.title: PoE Monitor
tools.description: Reads real-time Power-over-Ethernet (PoE) monitor data for one or more ethernet interfaces — PoE-out status, voltage, current, and power. Runs `/interface ethernet poe monitor <interfaces> once`.

interfaces.description: Comma-separated ethernet interface name(s), e.g. 'ether1' or 'ether9,ether10'

tools.name: list_poe
tools.title: List PoE
tools.description: Lists the Power-over-Ethernet (PoE) configuration of PoE-capable ethernet interfaces (PoE-out mode, priority). Runs `/interface ethernet poe print`.

tools.name: get_poe_settings
tools.title: PoE Settings
tools.description: Gets the detailed PoE-out settings of a specific ethernet interface (mode, priority, voltage, thresholds). Runs `/interface ethernet poe print detail where name=<name>`.

name.description: Exact ethernet interface name, e.g. 'ether1'

tools.name: get_route_cache
tools.title: Get Route Cache
tools.description: Shows the route/forwarding cache. Version-aware: on RouterOS v6 it reads the real route cache (`/ip route cache`); on v7+ — which removed the separate cache — it returns the active forwarding table (FIB), i.e. `/ip route` entries with active=yes, the closest equivalent.

tools.name: flush_route_cache
tools.title: Flush Route Cache
tools.description: Flushes the route cache (`/ip route cache flush`). Version-aware: on RouterOS v6 it flushes the cache; on v7+ there is no separate cache, so it reports a no-op (the FIB is rebuilt from /ip route directly).

tools.name: add_default_route
tools.title: Add Default Route
tools.description: Adds a default route.

tools.name: add_blackhole_route
tools.title: Add Blackhole Route
tools.description: Adds a blackhole route.

tools.name: get_route_statistics
tools.title: Route Statistics
tools.description: Gets routing table statistics.

tools.name: create_filter_rule
tools.title: Create Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Creates a firewall filter rule in the specified chain on the MikroTik device. connection_state: comma-separated e.g. "established,related,new,invalid". limit: RouterOS rate/burst string e.g. "10,5:packet" or "10/1s:packet". tcp_flags: RouterOS flag expression e.g. "syn,!ack". place_before: rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3".

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3"

tools.name: list_filter_rules
tools.title: List Firewall Filter Rules
tools.description: Lists firewall filter rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_filter_rule
tools.title: Get Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific firewall filter rule. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0".

rule_id.description: Rule ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0"

tools.name: update_filter_rule
tools.title: Update Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Updates an existing firewall filter rule on the MikroTik device. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0". connection_state: comma-separated e.g. "established,related". limit: RouterOS rate string e.g. "10,5:packet". tcp_flags: RouterOS flag expression e.g. "syn,!ack". Pass "" to clear an optional field (e.g. src_address="").

tools.name: remove_filter_rule
tools.title: Remove Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Removes a firewall filter rule from the MikroTik device. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0".

tools.name: move_filter_rule
tools.title: Move Filter Rule
tools.description: Moves a firewall filter rule to a different position in the chain. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0". destination: 0-based target position index.

tools.name: enable_filter_rule
tools.title: Enable Filter Rule
tools.description: Enables a firewall filter rule.

tools.name: disable_filter_rule
tools.title: Disable Filter Rule
tools.description: Disables a firewall filter rule.

tools.name: create_basic_firewall_setup
tools.title: Create Basic Firewall Setup
tools.description: Creates a basic firewall setup with common security rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_nat_rule
tools.title: Create NAT Rule
tools.description: Creates a NAT rule (srcnat or dstnat) on the MikroTik device. to_addresses: single IP or range e.g. "10.0.0.1" or "10.0.0.1-10.0.0.10". to_ports: single port or range e.g. "8080" or "8080-8090". place_before: rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3".

to_ports.description: Single port or range e.g. "8080" or "8080-8090"

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3"

tools.name: add_wireguard_peer
tools.title: Add WireGuard Peer
tools.description:
Adds a WireGuard peer (with public key and allowed addresses) to an interface on the MikroTik device.

Notes:
  allowed_address: CIDR, comma-separated for multiple e.g. "10.0.0.2/32" or "10.0.0.0/24,192.168.0.0/24"
  endpoint_address: remote host IP or hostname e.g. "203.0.113.1"
  persistent_keepalive: seconds as string e.g. "25"

allowed_address.description: CIDR, comma-separated for multiple e.g. "10.0.0.2/32" or "10.0.0.0/24,192.168.0.0/24"

endpoint_address.description: remote host IP or hostname e.g. "203.0.113.1"

tools.title: Get WireGuard Peer
tools.description:

Notes:
  peer_id: "*N" or "N" from list output e.g. "*2"

tools.name: update_wireguard_peer
tools.title: Update WireGuard Peer
tools.description:
Updates an existing WireGuard peer's allowed addresses, endpoint, keepalive, or enabled state.

Notes:
  peer_id: "*N" or "N" from list output e.g. "*2"
  allowed_address: CIDR, comma-separated e.g. "10.0.0.2/32" or "10.0.0.0/24,192.168.0.0/24"
  persistent_keepalive: seconds as string e.g. "25"
  Pass "" for endpoint_address or preshared_key to clear them.

tools.name: remove_wireguard_peer
tools.title: Remove WireGuard Peer
tools.description:

Notes:
  peer_id: "*N" or "N" from list output e.g. "*2"

tools.name: enable_wireguard_peer
tools.title: Enable WireGuard Peer
tools.description:

  peer_id: "*N" or "N" from list output e.g. "*2"

tools.name: disable_wireguard_peer
tools.title: Disable WireGuard Peer

tools.title: List IPsec Proposals

tools.name: get_ipsec_proposal
tools.title: Get IPsec Proposal
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPsec proposal.

tools.name: update_ipsec_proposal
tools.title: Update IPsec Proposal
tools.description: Updates an existing IPsec phase-2 proposal's settings.

name.description: Current name of the IPsec proposal to update

tools.name: remove_ipsec_proposal
tools.title: Remove IPsec Proposal
tools.description: Removes an IPsec proposal from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_ipsec_policy
tools.title: Create IPsec Policy
tools.description: Creates an IPsec policy defining which traffic is secured and how.

peer.description: Peer name this policy applies to

tools.name: list_ipsec_policies
tools.title: List IPsec Policies
tools.description: Lists IPsec policies on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_ipsec_policy
tools.title: Remove IPsec Policy
tools.description: Removes an IPsec policy by its internal ID (e.g. '*1').

tools.name: get_ipsec_active_peers
tools.title: Get IPsec Active Peers
tools.description: Shows currently established IPsec peers (active IKE sessions).

tools.name: get_ipsec_installed_sa
tools.title: Get IPsec Installed SAs
tools.description: Shows installed IPsec security associations (SAs).

tools.name: flush_ipsec_installed_sa
tools.title: Flush IPsec Installed SAs
tools.description: Flushes all installed IPsec security associations, forcing tunnels to rekey.

tools.name: get_ipsec_statistics
tools.title: Get IPsec Statistics
tools.description: Shows IPsec subsystem statistics and counters.

tools.name: create_ppp_profile
tools.title: Create PPP Profile
tools.description: Creates a PPP profile on the MikroTik device. Profiles define address assignment, DNS, encryption, and rate limits shared by L2TP/PPTP/SSTP/OpenVPN sessions.

name.description: Name for the new PPP profile

local_address.description: Server-side tunnel IP or pool name

remote_address.description: Client IP or address pool name

only_one.description: Allow only one session per user

tools.name: list_ppp_profiles
tools.title: List PPP Profiles
tools.description: Lists PPP profiles on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ppp_profile
tools.title: Get PPP Profile
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific PPP profile.

tools.name: update_ppp_profile
tools.title: Update PPP Profile
tools.description: Updates an existing PPP profile's settings.

name.description: Current name of the PPP profile to update

tools.name: remove_ppp_profile
tools.title: Remove PPP Profile
tools.description: Removes a PPP profile from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_ppp_secret
tools.title: Create PPP Secret
tools.description: Creates a PPP secret (VPN user account) on the MikroTik device. Used by L2TP/PPTP/SSTP/OpenVPN/PPPoE servers for client authentication.

name.description: Username for the secret

tools.name: list_ppp_secrets
tools.title: List PPP Secrets
tools.description: Lists PPP secrets (VPN user accounts) on the MikroTik device. Passwords are redacted.

tools.name: get_ppp_secret
tools.title: Get PPP Secret
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific PPP secret. The password is redacted.

tools.name: update_ppp_secret
tools.title: Update PPP Secret
tools.description: Updates an existing PPP secret's settings. The password is redacted in output.

name.description: Current username of the secret to update

tools.name: remove_ppp_secret
tools.title: Remove PPP Secret
tools.description: Removes a PPP secret (VPN user account) from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ppp_active
tools.title: Active PPP Sessions
tools.description: Lists currently active PPP sessions (connected VPN clients).

tools.name: disconnect_ppp_active
tools.title: Disconnect PPP Session
tools.description: Disconnects an active PPP session by username.

name.description: Username of the active session to disconnect

tools.name: get_l2tp_server
tools.title: Get L2TP Server
tools.description: Gets the L2TP server configuration on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: set_l2tp_server
tools.title: Set L2TP Server
tools.description: Configures the L2TP server. For L2TP/IPsec road-warrior setups, set use_ipsec='required' and supply ipsec_secret.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the L2TP server

tools.name: create_l2tp_client
tools.title: Create L2TP Client
tools.description: Creates an L2TP client interface that dials out to a remote L2TP server.

name.description: Name for the new L2TP client interface

tools.name: list_l2tp_clients
tools.title: List L2TP Clients
tools.description: Lists L2TP client interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_l2tp_client
tools.title: Get L2TP Client
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific L2TP client. The password is redacted.

tools.name: remove_l2tp_client
tools.title: Remove L2TP Client
tools.description: Removes an L2TP client interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_l2tp_client
tools.title: Enable L2TP Client
tools.description: Enables an L2TP client interface.

tools.name: disable_l2tp_client
tools.title: Disable L2TP Client
tools.description: Disables an L2TP client interface.

tools.name: get_pptp_server
tools.title: Get PPTP Server
tools.description: Gets the PPTP server configuration. NOTE: PPTP is legacy/weak — prefer L2TP/IPsec, SSTP, or WireGuard.

tools.name: set_pptp_server
tools.title: Set PPTP Server
tools.description: Configures the PPTP server. NOTE: PPTP is legacy/weak — prefer L2TP/IPsec, SSTP, or WireGuard for new deployments.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the PPTP server

tools.name: create_pptp_client
tools.title: Create PPTP Client
tools.description: Creates a PPTP client interface that dials out to a remote PPTP server. NOTE: PPTP is legacy/weak — prefer L2TP/IPsec, SSTP, or WireGuard.

name.description: Name for the new PPTP client interface

tools.name: list_pptp_clients
tools.title: List PPTP Clients
tools.description: Lists PPTP client interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_pptp_client
tools.title: Get PPTP Client
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific PPTP client. The password is redacted.

tools.name: remove_pptp_client
tools.title: Remove PPTP Client
tools.description: Removes a PPTP client interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_sstp_server
tools.title: Get SSTP Server
tools.description: Gets the SSTP server (TLS) configuration on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: set_sstp_server
tools.title: Set SSTP Server
tools.description: Configures the SSTP server (TLS-based VPN). Requires a TLS certificate.

tools.name: create_sstp_client
tools.title: Create SSTP Client
tools.description: Creates an SSTP client interface connecting to a remote SSTP server over TLS.

name.description: Name for the new SSTP client interface

connect_to.description: Remote SSTP server address (host:port or IP)

tools.name: list_sstp_clients
tools.title: List SSTP Clients
tools.description: Lists SSTP client interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_sstp_client
tools.title: Get SSTP Client
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific SSTP client interface.

tools.name: remove_sstp_client
tools.title: Remove SSTP Client
tools.description: Removes an SSTP client interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ovpn_server
tools.title: Get OpenVPN Server
tools.description: Gets the OpenVPN (OVPN) server configuration on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: set_ovpn_server
tools.title: Set OpenVPN Server
tools.description: Configures the OpenVPN (OVPN) server. RouterOS 7 supports both UDP and TCP.

tools.name: create_ovpn_client
tools.title: Create OpenVPN Client
tools.description: Creates an OpenVPN (OVPN) client interface connecting to a remote OpenVPN server.

name.description: Name for the new OpenVPN client interface

tools.name: list_ovpn_clients
tools.title: List OpenVPN Clients
tools.description: Lists OpenVPN (OVPN) client interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ovpn_client
tools.title: Get OpenVPN Client
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific OpenVPN client interface.

tools.name: remove_ovpn_client
tools.title: Remove OpenVPN Client
tools.description: Removes an OpenVPN client interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_ovpn_client
tools.title: Enable OpenVPN Client
tools.description: Enables an OpenVPN client interface.

tools.name: disable_ovpn_client
tools.title: Disable OpenVPN Client
tools.description: Disables an OpenVPN client interface.

tools.name: create_gre_tunnel
tools.title: Create GRE Tunnel
tools.description: Creates a GRE (Generic Routing Encapsulation) L3 tunnel interface on the MikroTik device.

name.description: Name for the new GRE tunnel interface, e.g. 'gre-to-hq'

clamp_tcp_mss.description: Clamp TCP MSS to the tunnel MTU

tools.name: list_gre_tunnels
tools.title: List GRE Tunnels
tools.description: Lists GRE tunnel interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_gre_tunnel
tools.title: Get GRE Tunnel
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific GRE tunnel interface.

tools.name: remove_gre_tunnel
tools.title: Remove GRE Tunnel
tools.description: Removes a GRE tunnel interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_ipip_tunnel
tools.title: Create IPIP Tunnel
tools.description: Creates an IPIP (IP-in-IP) L3 tunnel interface on the MikroTik device.

name.description: Name for the new IPIP tunnel interface, e.g. 'ipip-to-hq'

tools.name: list_ipip_tunnels
tools.title: List IPIP Tunnels
tools.description: Lists IPIP tunnel interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipip_tunnel
tools.title: Get IPIP Tunnel
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPIP tunnel interface.

tools.name: remove_ipip_tunnel
tools.title: Remove IPIP Tunnel
tools.description: Removes an IPIP tunnel interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_eoip_tunnel
tools.title: Create EoIP Tunnel
tools.description: Creates an EoIP (Ethernet over IP) L2 tunnel interface on the MikroTik device. Bridgeable; each tunnel needs a unique tunnel-id matching the remote peer.

name.description: Name for the new EoIP tunnel interface, e.g. 'eoip-to-hq'

tunnel_id.description: Unique tunnel ID, must match on both peers

tools.name: list_eoip_tunnels
tools.title: List EoIP Tunnels
tools.description: Lists EoIP tunnel interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_eoip_tunnel
tools.title: Get EoIP Tunnel
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific EoIP tunnel interface.

tools.name: remove_eoip_tunnel
tools.title: Remove EoIP Tunnel
tools.description: Removes an EoIP tunnel interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_vxlan_tunnel
tools.title: Create VXLAN Tunnel
tools.description: Creates a VXLAN (Virtual Extensible LAN) L2 overlay interface on the MikroTik device.

name.description: Name for the new VXLAN interface, e.g. 'vxlan1'

tools.name: list_vxlan_tunnels
tools.title: List VXLAN Tunnels
tools.description: Lists VXLAN interfaces on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_vxlan_tunnel
tools.title: Get VXLAN Tunnel
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific VXLAN interface.

tools.name: remove_vxlan_tunnel
tools.title: Remove VXLAN Tunnel
tools.description: Removes a VXLAN interface from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_radius_server
tools.title: Add RADIUS Server
tools.description: Adds a RADIUS server entry that the router uses to authenticate clients.

address.description: RADIUS server IP address or hostname

secret.description: Shared secret used with the RADIUS server

tools.name: list_radius_servers
tools.title: List RADIUS Servers
tools.description: Lists configured RADIUS servers.

tools.name: get_radius_server
tools.title: Get RADIUS Server
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific RADIUS server by its internal id.

tools.name: update_radius_server
tools.title: Update RADIUS Server
tools.description: Updates an existing RADIUS server entry.

tools.name: remove_radius_server
tools.title: Remove RADIUS Server
tools.description: Removes a RADIUS server entry.

tools.name: enable_radius_server
tools.title: Enable RADIUS Server
tools.description: Enables a RADIUS server entry.

tools.name: disable_radius_server
tools.title: Disable RADIUS Server
tools.description: Disables a RADIUS server entry.

tools.name: get_radius_incoming
tools.title: Get RADIUS Incoming
tools.description: Gets the RADIUS incoming (Change of Authorization / CoA) settings.

tools.name: set_radius_incoming
tools.title: Set RADIUS Incoming
tools.description: Configures RADIUS incoming (Change of Authorization / CoA) settings.

accept.description: Whether to accept incoming CoA requests

port.description: UDP port to listen on for CoA requests

tools.name: reset_radius_counters
tools.title: Reset RADIUS Counters
tools.description: Resets the RADIUS request/response counters.

tools.name: get_user_manager_settings
tools.title: Get User Manager Settings
tools.description: Gets the User Manager settings (enabled, certificate, use-profiles).

tools.name: set_user_manager_settings
tools.title: Set User Manager Settings
tools.description: Updates the User Manager settings.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the User Manager server

certificate.description: TLS certificate name for RADIUS over TLS

tools.name: add_user_manager_user
tools.title: Add User Manager User
tools.description: Adds a user to the User Manager RADIUS database.

name.description: Login name for the user

tools.name: list_user_manager_users
tools.title: List User Manager Users
tools.description: Lists users in the User Manager RADIUS database.

tools.name: get_user_manager_user
tools.title: Get User Manager User
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific User Manager user.

tools.name: update_user_manager_user
tools.title: Update User Manager User
tools.description: Updates an existing User Manager user.

name.description: Current name of the user to update

tools.name: remove_user_manager_user
tools.title: Remove User Manager User
tools.description: Removes a user from the User Manager RADIUS database.

tools.name: add_user_manager_profile
tools.title: Add User Manager Profile
tools.description: Adds a User Manager profile (a billing/service plan).

name.description: Profile name

tools.name: list_user_manager_profiles
tools.title: List User Manager Profiles
tools.description: Lists User Manager profiles.

tools.name: remove_user_manager_profile
tools.title: Remove User Manager Profile
tools.description: Removes a User Manager profile.

tools.name: assign_user_manager_profile
tools.title: Assign User Manager Profile
tools.description: Assigns a profile to a User Manager user.

user.description: User to assign the profile to

tools.name: list_user_manager_user_profiles
tools.title: List User Manager User-Profiles
tools.description: Lists User Manager user-profile assignments.

tools.name: add_user_manager_router
tools.title: Add User Manager Router
tools.description: Adds a RADIUS client (router/NAS) that authenticates against User Manager.

name.description: Friendly name for the RADIUS client

address.description: IP address of the RADIUS client

shared_secret.description: Shared secret for the RADIUS client

tools.name: list_user_manager_routers
tools.title: List User Manager Routers
tools.description: Lists RADIUS clients (routers/NAS) configured in User Manager.

tools.name: remove_user_manager_router
tools.title: Remove User Manager Router
tools.description: Removes a RADIUS client (router/NAS) from User Manager.

tools.name: add_user_manager_limitation
tools.title: Add User Manager Limitation
tools.description: Adds a User Manager limitation (rate/transfer/uptime limits).

name.description: Limitation name

tools.name: list_user_manager_limitations
tools.title: List User Manager Limitations
tools.description: Lists User Manager limitations.

tools.name: remove_user_manager_limitation
tools.title: Remove User Manager Limitation
tools.description: Removes a User Manager limitation.

tools.name: list_user_manager_sessions
tools.title: List User Manager Sessions
tools.description: Lists User Manager accounting sessions.

tools.name: create_queue_type
tools.title: Create Queue Type
tools.description: Creates a queue type (qdisc). kind selects the discipline (cake, fq-codel, sfq, red, pcq, pfifo, bfifo); remaining params are per-discipline options.

tools.name: list_queue_types
tools.title: List Queue Types
tools.description: Lists queue types on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_queue_type
tools.title: Get Queue Type
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific queue type.

tools.name: update_queue_type
tools.title: Update Queue Type
tools.description: Updates an existing queue type's discipline-specific settings.

tools.name: remove_queue_type
tools.title: Remove Queue Type
tools.description: Removes a queue type from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_queue_tree
tools.title: Create Queue Tree
tools.description: Creates a hierarchical queue tree entry attached to a parent interface or queue.

parent.description: Interface name e.g. "ether1" or parent queue name

tools.name: list_queue_trees
tools.title: List Queue Trees
tools.description: Lists queue trees on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_queue_tree
tools.title: Get Queue Tree
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific queue tree.

tools.name: update_queue_tree
tools.title: Update Queue Tree
tools.description: Updates an existing queue tree entry (bandwidth limits, parent, priority, etc.).

tools.name: remove_queue_tree
tools.title: Remove Queue Tree
tools.description: Removes a queue tree from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_queue_tree
tools.title: Enable Queue Tree
tools.description: Enables a queue tree.

tools.name: disable_queue_tree
tools.title: Disable Queue Tree
tools.description: Disables a queue tree.

tools.name: create_simple_queue
tools.title: Create Simple Queue
tools.description: Creates a simple queue to rate-limit a target address or interface.

max_limit.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

limit_at.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

burst_limit.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

burst_threshold.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

tools.name: list_simple_queues
tools.title: List Simple Queues
tools.description: Lists simple queues on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_simple_queue
tools.title: Get Simple Queue
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific simple queue.

tools.name: update_simple_queue
tools.title: Update Simple Queue
tools.description: Updates an existing simple queue's rate limits, target, or scheduling settings.

max_limit.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

limit_at.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

burst_limit.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

burst_threshold.description: Upload/download bandwidth as "UL/DL" e.g. "10M/10M", or single value e.g. "10M"

tools.name: remove_simple_queue
tools.title: Remove Simple Queue
tools.description: Removes a simple queue from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_simple_queue
tools.title: Enable Simple Queue
tools.description: Enables a simple queue.

tools.name: disable_simple_queue
tools.title: Disable Simple Queue
tools.description: Disables a simple queue.

tools.name: list_mikrotik_devices
tools.title: List Devices
tools.description: Lists the configured MikroTik devices (name, host, description) and which is the default. Pass a device's name as the `device` argument on any other tool to target it. Secrets are never shown.

tools.name: get_system_identity
tools.title: Get Identity
tools.description: Gets the system identity (hostname) of the MikroTik device.

tools.name: set_system_identity
tools.title: Set Identity
tools.description: Sets the system identity (hostname) of the MikroTik device.

name.description: New system identity / hostname

tools.name: get_system_resources
tools.title: Get Resources
tools.description: Gets system resource information (CPU, memory, uptime, board name, version).

tools.name: get_system_health
tools.title: Get Health
tools.description: Gets system health sensor readings (voltage, temperature, fans). Some devices have no sensors.

tools.name: get_routerboard
tools.title: Get RouterBOARD
tools.description: Gets RouterBOARD hardware information (model, serial, firmware).

tools.name: get_system_clock
tools.title: Get Clock
tools.description: Gets the current system clock, date, and time-zone settings.

tools.name: set_system_clock
tools.title: Set Clock
tools.description: Sets system clock settings. Provide at least one of time-zone, date, or time.

tools.name: get_ntp_client
tools.title: Get NTP Client
tools.description: Gets the NTP client configuration and synchronization status.

tools.name: set_ntp_client
tools.title: Set NTP Client
tools.description: Configures the NTP client (enable/disable and server list).

enabled.description: Enable or disable the NTP client

tools.name: get_installed_packages
tools.title: List Packages
tools.description: Lists installed software packages and their versions.

tools.name: check_for_updates
tools.title: Check Updates
tools.description: Checks for available RouterOS updates on the configured update channel.

tools.name: get_system_history
tools.title: Get History
tools.description: Gets the system change history (recent configuration actions).

tools.name: reboot_system
tools.title: Reboot System
tools.description: Reboots the MikroTik device. Requires confirm=true; the connection will drop.

confirm.description: Must be true to actually reboot the device

tools.name: shutdown_system
tools.title: Shutdown System
tools.description: Shuts down the MikroTik device. Requires confirm=true; the connection will drop.

confirm.description: Must be true to actually shut down the device

tools.name: list_system_console
tools.title: List Console
tools.description: Lists the system console sessions/ports (`/system console`).

tools.name: list_leds
tools.title: List LEDs
tools.description: Lists the configured LEDs and their triggers (`/system leds`).

tools.name: get_leds_settings
tools.title: Get LED Settings
tools.description: Gets the global LED settings (`/system leds settings`).

tools.name: set_leds_settings
tools.title: Set LED Settings
tools.description: Updates the global LED settings, e.g. dark-mode scheduling via all-leds-off.

all_leds_off.description: When to turn all LEDs off (dark mode)

tools.name: get_license
tools.title: Get License
tools.description: Gets the RouterOS license (`/system license`). On CHR shows the license level and deadline; on RouterBOARD the menu may differ or be absent.

tools.name: get_note
tools.title: Get Note
tools.description: Gets the system note shown at login (`/system note`).

tools.name: set_note
tools.title: Set Note
tools.description: Sets the system note and whether it is displayed at login.

tools.name: get_ntp_server
tools.title: Get NTP Server
tools.description: Gets the NTP server configuration (`/system ntp server`, RouterOS 7).

tools.name: set_ntp_server
tools.title: Set NTP Server
tools.description: Configures the built-in NTP server (enable, broadcast/multicast/manycast modes).

enabled.description: Enable or disable the NTP server

tools.name: change_password
tools.title: Change Password
tools.description: Changes the current user's login password (`/password`). The old and new passwords are never echoed back in the result.

tools.name: list_ports
tools.title: List Serial Ports
tools.description: Lists the device's serial ports (`/port`).

tools.name: get_port
tools.title: Get Serial Port
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific serial port.

name.description: Serial port name, e.g. 'serial0'

tools.name: set_port
tools.title: Set Serial Port
tools.description: Updates a serial port's line settings (baud rate, data/stop bits, parity, flow control).

name.description: Serial port name to update

tools.name: get_regulatory
tools.title: Get Regulatory
tools.description: Surfaces the wireless regulatory/country domain. RouterOS has no `/system regulatory` menu; this reads `/interface wifi radio` (wifiwave2), which exposes the country and regulatory settings of the radios.

tools.name: reset_configuration
tools.title: Reset Configuration
tools.description: Factory-resets the device configuration (`/system reset-configuration`). Requires confirm=true; the device reboots into a default configuration and the connection will drop.

confirm.description: Must be true to actually ERASE the configuration

keep_users.description: Keep existing user accounts after reset

no_defaults.description: Do not load the default configuration

skip_backup.description: Skip the automatic backup before reset

run_after_reset.description: Script file to run after reset

tools.name: list_special_login
tools.title: List Special Login
tools.description: Lists special-login entries, e.g. serial-console auto-login (`/system special-login`).

tools.name: get_watchdog
tools.title: Get Watchdog
tools.description: Gets the hardware/software watchdog configuration (`/system watchdog`).

tools.name: set_watchdog
tools.title: Set Watchdog
tools.description: Configures the watchdog timer and the host it pings to detect a hung device.

watch_address.description: Address to ping; reboot if unreachable

automatic_supout.description: Generate a supout.rif on software failure

tools.name: ping
tools.title: Ping
tools.description: Sends ICMP echo requests to a host. Output reflects a single bounded run of `count` packets (1-100); it does not stream continuously.

address.description: Target host or IP to ping

tools.name: traceroute
tools.title: Traceroute
tools.description: Traces the network path to a host. traceroute can stream; output reflects a bounded run of `count` probes (keep count small).

address.description: Target host or IP to trace

count.description: Number of probes per hop (keep small to bound the run)

tools.name: bandwidth_test
tools.title: Bandwidth Test
tools.description: Runs a throughput test against a target that must be running a bandwidth-test server. Output reflects a bounded run of `duration` seconds; it does not run indefinitely.

tools.name: resolve_dns
tools.title: Resolve DNS
tools.description: Resolves a DNS name to an address using the device's configured resolver.

name.description: DNS name to resolve, e.g. 'example.com'

server.description: Specific DNS server to query (informational; :resolve uses the system resolver)

tools.name: add_netwatch
tools.title: Add Netwatch
tools.description: Adds a netwatch entry that monitors a host and optionally runs scripts on up/down transitions.

up_script.description: Script to run when the host comes up

down_script.description: Script to run when the host goes down

tools.name: list_netwatch
tools.title: List Netwatch
tools.description: Lists netwatch host-monitoring entries.

tools.name: get_netwatch
tools.title: Get Netwatch
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific netwatch entry.

tools.name: remove_netwatch
tools.title: Remove Netwatch
tools.description: Removes a netwatch entry by host.

tools.name: create_scheduler
tools.title: Create Scheduler
tools.description: Creates a scheduled task on the MikroTik device that runs an on-event script at an interval or start time.

name.description: Name for the scheduler entry

on_event.description: Script source to run on event (may contain spaces/semicolons)

tools.name: list_schedulers
tools.title: List Schedulers
tools.description: Lists scheduled tasks on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_scheduler
tools.title: Get Scheduler
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific scheduler entry.

tools.name: remove_scheduler
tools.title: Remove Scheduler
tools.description: Removes a scheduled task from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_scheduler
tools.title: Enable Scheduler
tools.description: Enables a scheduled task on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: disable_scheduler
tools.title: Disable Scheduler
tools.description: Disables a scheduled task on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_script
tools.title: Add Script
tools.description: Adds a named script to the MikroTik device's script repository.

name.description: Name for the script

dont_require_permissions.description: Run without checking the policy permissions of the caller

tools.name: list_scripts
tools.title: List Scripts
tools.description: Lists scripts in the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: remove_script
tools.title: Remove Script
tools.description: Removes a script from the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: run_script
tools.title: Run Script
tools.description: Runs a named script from the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: add_user
tools.title: Add User
tools.description: Adds a user to MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_users
tools.title: List Users
tools.description: Lists users on MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_user
tools.title: Get User
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific user.

tools.name: update_user
tools.title: Update User
tools.description: Updates a user.

tools.name: remove_user
tools.title: Remove User
tools.description: Removes a user.

tools.name: disable_user
tools.title: Disable User
tools.description: Disables a user.

tools.name: enable_user
tools.title: Enable User
tools.description: Enables a user.

tools.name: add_user_group
tools.title: Add User Group
tools.description: Adds a user group.

tools.name: list_user_groups
tools.title: List User Groups
tools.description: Lists user groups on MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_user_group
tools.title: Get User Group
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific user group.

tools.name: update_user_group
tools.title: Update User Group
tools.description: Updates a user group.

tools.name: remove_user_group
tools.title: Remove User Group
tools.description: Removes a user group.

tools.name: get_active_users
tools.title: Active Users
tools.description: Gets currently active/logged-in users.

tools.name: disconnect_user
tools.title: Disconnect User
tools.description: Disconnects an active user session.

tools.name: export_user_config
tools.title: Export User Config
tools.description: Exports user configuration to a file.

tools.name: set_user_ssh_keys
tools.title: Set User SSH Keys
tools.description: Sets SSH keys for a specific user.

tools.name: list_user_ssh_keys
tools.title: List User SSH Keys
tools.description: Lists SSH keys for a specific user.

tools.name: remove_user_ssh_key
tools.title: Remove User SSH Key
tools.description: Removes an SSH key.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.title: Get Logs
tools.description: Gets logs from the MikroTik device with optional topic, time, and message filters.

tools.name: get_logs_by_severity
tools.title: Get Logs by Severity
tools.description: Gets logs filtered by severity level (debug/info/warning/error/critical).

tools.name: get_logs_by_topic
tools.title: Get Logs by Topic
tools.description: Gets logs for a specific topic/facility (system, dhcp, interface, firewall, etc.).

tools.name: search_logs
tools.title: Search Logs
tools.description: Searches log messages for a specific term.

tools.name: get_system_events
tools.title: System Events
tools.description: Gets system-related log events (login, reboot, config-change, etc.).

tools.name: get_security_logs
tools.title: Security Logs
tools.description: Gets security-related log entries (login failures, blocked connections, etc.).

tools.name: clear_logs
tools.title: Clear Logs
tools.description: Clears all logs from the MikroTik device. This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: get_log_statistics
tools.title: Log Statistics
tools.description: Gets log entry counts by topic and severity from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: export_logs
tools.title: Export Logs
tools.description: Exports logs to a file on the MikroTik device with optional topic and time filters.

tools.name: monitor_logs
tools.title: Monitor Logs
tools.description: Monitors MikroTik logs in near-real-time for a limited duration (max 60s).

tools.name: add_logging_rule
tools.title: Add Logging Rule
tools.description: Adds a system logging rule that routes log messages matching the given topics to a logging action.

prefix.description: Text prepended to each matching log message

tools.name: list_logging_rules
tools.title: List Logging Rules
tools.description: Lists system logging rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_logging_rule
tools.title: Remove Logging Rule
tools.description: Removes a system logging rule by its internal id.

rule_id.description: Internal .id of the logging rule, e.g. '*1'

tools.name: add_logging_action
tools.title: Add Logging Action
tools.description: Adds a system logging action defining where matching log messages are sent (memory, disk, echo, remote syslog or email).

name.description: Name for the new logging action

tools.name: list_logging_actions
tools.title: List Logging Actions
tools.description: Lists system logging actions on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_logging_action
tools.title: Remove Logging Action
tools.description: Removes a system logging action by name.

name.description: Name of the logging action to remove

tools.name: create_backup
tools.title: Create Backup
tools.description: Creates a system backup on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_backups
tools.title: List Backups
tools.description: Lists backup files on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_export
tools.title: Create Config Export
tools.description: Creates a configuration export file (rsc/json/xml) on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: export_section
tools.title: Export Config Section
tools.description: Exports a specific RouterOS configuration section to a file. section: RouterOS path without leading slash e.g. "ip address", "interface vlan", "ip firewall filter", "ip firewall nat", "queue simple".

tools.name: download_file
tools.title: Download File
tools.description: Downloads a backup or export file from the MikroTik device as base64-encoded content.

tools.name: upload_file
tools.title: Upload File
tools.description: Uploads a base64-encoded file to the MikroTik device (for restore operations).

tools.name: restore_backup
tools.title: Restore Backup
tools.description: Restores a system backup on the MikroTik device; triggers a reboot.

tools.name: import_configuration
tools.title: Import Configuration
tools.description: Imports and executes a RouterOS configuration script (.rsc file) on the device.

tools.name: remove_file
tools.title: Remove File
tools.description: Removes a file from the MikroTik device filesystem.

tools.name: backup_info
tools.title: Backup File Info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a backup file on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_disks
tools.title: List Disks
tools.description: Lists storage disks (USB, NVMe, internal flash) attached to the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_disk
tools.title: Get Disk
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific disk.

name.description: Disk name, e.g. 'disk1'

tools.name: format_disk
tools.title: Format Disk
tools.description: Formats (ERASES) a disk on the MikroTik device. This destroys all data on the disk. Requires confirm=true.

name.description: Disk name to format, e.g. 'disk1'

confirm.description: Must be true to actually erase the disk

tools.name: safe_mode_status
tools.title: Safe Mode Status
tools.description: Returns whether MikroTik Safe Mode is currently active (for the targeted device).

tools.name: enable_safe_mode
tools.title: Enable Safe Mode
tools.description: Activates MikroTik Safe Mode on the targeted device; changes are held in memory and auto-reverted on disconnect until committed.

tools.name: commit_safe_mode
tools.title: Commit Safe Mode
tools.description: Commits all pending Safe Mode changes on the targeted device to persistent storage and exits Safe Mode.

tools.name: rollback_safe_mode
tools.title: Rollback Safe Mode
tools.description: Discards all pending Safe Mode changes on the targeted device by closing the SSH session, triggering automatic rollback.
